Create a 2D contour/heatmap from a 3D surface

Source: R/surf-contour.R
surf_contour.Rd

Projects the 3D prediction surface into a 2D contour plot or filled heatmap. Useful for publication-ready static figures.

Usage

surf_contour(
  object,
  type = c("contour", "heatmap"),
  nlevels = 10L,
  colourscale = "Viridis",
  interactive = TRUE,
  ...
)

Arguments

object

An effectsurf object.

type

Character. "contour" (default) for contour lines, "heatmap" for a filled heatmap.

nlevels

Integer. Number of contour levels. Default 10.

colourscale

Character. Colour palette name for fill. Default "Viridis".

interactive

Logical. If TRUE (default), returns an interactive Plotly widget. If FALSE and ggplot2 is available, returns a ggplot.

...

Additional arguments passed to Plotly or ggplot functions.

Value

A plotly widget or ggplot object.

Examples

# \donttest{
if (requireNamespace("mgcv", quietly = TRUE)) {
  model <- mgcv::gam(mpg ~ s(wt) + s(hp), data = mtcars)
  es <- surf_prediction(model, x = "wt", y = "hp",
                        x_length = 30, y_length = 30)
  surf_contour(es, type = "heatmap")
}


# }
